title: Chaenorhinum grandiflorum
taxonomy: Chaenorhinum grandiflorum is a species of flowering plant in the family Scrophulariaceae. This plant is native to the Mediterranean region and is a member of the genus Chaenorhinum. Chaenorhinum grandiflorum is often classified as a type of snapdragon or a toadflax due to its appearance and growth habits. It is a small, annual or biennial plant that produces small, fragrant flowers in shades of pink, purple, or white. The plant's classification is further supported by its morphological characteristics, which align with those of other species in the Scrophulariaceae family.

intro: Chaenorhinum grandiflorum, also known as snake flower or small toadflax, is a perennial herb belonging to the borage family (Boraginaceae). The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its potential to treat anxiety and insomnia due to its sedative and calming effects. Chaenorhinum grandiflorum contains bioactive constituents such as alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties.
